Current Cohort.name: Jeenu Pawar
Current Cohort.focus: Empowering Phase - Pardhi communities through education
Current Cohort.about: Education facilities in small villages were extremely restricted. The main impediment to rural education is uneducated parents, poor financial situation, lack of participation in education, and ignorance about educational benefits. Children and adolescents are the most affected by this condition. Shri Rudra Welfare Foundation, Nagpur creates the center in rural village which is known as "Anandghar - A Place of Joy". A spot where children will spend their time happily doing fun things and learning life skills. They openly express their emotions and thoughts to the elderly without hesitation. They want them to be happy and learning during their child time. They studied the villages and chose the most affected areas for this reason. They are launching a two-hour regular program to teach enjoyable learning education to children of various ages. They use educational fun games, social media, activity-based teaching, and other methods to achieve this goal. We provide nutritious food on a regular basis for their overall health, such as fruits, nuts, chikki, dietary food, and so on. They link the youth through this center and have a good understanding of their issues by created a youth katta for this reason, where They provide them with career advice and solve their other problems. This aided them in launching a youth-oriented career initiative.

Current Cohort.name: Sheshraj Shisodiya
Current Cohort.focus: Empowering Phase - Pardhi communities through education
Current Cohort.about: Lahar is a youth-led collective from the Pardhi community that works to strengthen access to education and address identity-related challenges. Through creative and participatory methods—such as theatre, sports, libraries, and community meetings—Lahar engages children and youth to spark awareness and interest in education. The collective also tackles critical issues including child marriage, access to panchayat and judicial rights, and mental health. Lahar believes that true empowerment comes when the community itself recognizes its identity and stands up for its rights.

Current Cohort.name: Akram
Current Cohort.focus: Nurturing values through art and dialogue
Current Cohort.about: Kala Samvad Foundation works to foster values and drive positive social change through art and dialogue. Believing that every child and young person carries unique creativity, the foundation creates open and creative spaces—through dance, music, theatre, literature, and other art forms—where children and youth can express themselves and build confidence. Its workshops, cultural events, and dialogue programs aim to strengthen values of love, peace, equality, and fraternity, while connecting art with everyday life to inspire social awareness and transformation.

Current Cohort.name: Ohab Ansari
Current Cohort.focus: Taking education and dignity to every doorstep of Mir, Banjara & Fakir Community
Current Cohort.about:
Since 2018, Samavesh is working with four major nomadic tribes—Mir, Fakir, Banjara, and Od—living in temporary settlements across villages and slums in Gujarat. These communities face deep challenges, relying on begging and daily wage labor, with little access to education, healthcare, or basic services.

The initiative engages 312 families across six villages and three towns in Mehsana, Sabarkantha, and Banaskantha districts. Its focus is on expanding access to education, building awareness of rights, and fostering dignity and social inclusion for nomadic communities. They believe that true inclusion and transformation can only be achieved when education and dignity reach the most marginalized.

Current Cohort.name: Priti & Anil
Current Cohort.focus: Empowering nomadic lives through identity and education
Current Cohort.about: Registered in 2013, Samaj Uttan Seva Samiti works with Denotified and Nomadic Tribes (DNTs and NTs), among the most marginalized communities deprived of stable housing, education, and basic services. Its core focus is connecting children to mainstream education while also helping families secure legal identity documents and access government welfare schemes. Emerging from within the community itself, the organization fosters youth leadership—among both women and men—so they can advocate for their rights and drive change from within.

Current Cohort.name: Mehruddin
Current Cohort.focus: Smiles of learning, earning, and living well.
Current Cohort.about:
Project Smile works to ensure that children from economically disadvantaged families are not deprived of education by providing free learning support and study materials. Alongside this, the project empowers educated yet unemployed youth through skill development programs—run in collaboration with partners like Sanjhe Sapne and School for Programming—that build employable skills and connect them to job opportunities. In the area of health, Project Smile facilitates affordable diagnostic services and raises awareness about government welfare schemes, helping families access their rightful entitlements.

Its vision is simple yet powerful: every child educated, every youth employed, and every family healthy and aware.

Current Cohort.name: Dashrath
Current Cohort.focus: Nurturing dreams of Katkari children, one learning space at a time
Current Cohort.about: Sanvedan Kendra works with the Katkari nomadic tribe in Goa, one of the most marginalized communities in the region. The organization focuses on ensuring that Katkari children not only attend school but also thrive—through daily remedial classes in settlements, individualized support within schools, and residential learning camps that offer safe, inspiring spaces for growth. These efforts are helping improve learning outcomes, build confidence, and nurture aspirations—laying the foundation for lasting community transformation.

Current Cohort.name: Phoolchand
Current Cohort.focus: Lighting up dreams of tribal girls in Chhattisgarh.
Current Cohort.about: JUGNI works with tribal adolescent girls (ages 13–18) in remote areas of Chhattisgarh, where distance, household responsibilities, and gender inequality often push them out of school. Through its learning center—a vibrant space that serves as both a library and a hub for tailored programs—JUGNI helps girls reconnect with education while building awareness of their rights, leadership, and life skills. The initiative empowers every girl to pursue her studies, nurture her dreams, and step forward with confidence and dignity.

Current Cohort.name: Nirmala Jhala
Current Cohort.focus: Education that travels with the pastoralists
Current Cohort.about: Nirmala works with the Bharwad pastoralist families in the Jalgaon district of Maharashtra—communities displaced from Gujarat at the time of Independence and still living lives of constant movement. For them, even basic needs like food, shelter, and education are daily struggles. Through her initiative, Nirmala strives to bring education into their everyday lives—not just as a means of learning, but as a pathway to dignity, stability, and hope for a better future.

2024-25.name: Mukund Kale
2024-25.org: Spark Social Education and Welfare Society
2024-25.focus: Igniting Change and Community Action in Vadar Community
2024-25.about:
Established in 2016, Spark Foundation has been working to support underprivileged and aspiring students in rural areas by providing clothes, school uniforms, books, notebooks, and bags. Beyond education, the organization undertakes tree plantation drives on Gram Panchayat land, maintaining them consistently for two to three years to promote environmental sustainability.

During periods of drought, Spark addressed the drinking water shortage in a local school by collaboratively installing a borewell with the help of villagers—an example of their strong participatory approach.

The organization also focuses on student motivation, career guidance, and higher education support, organizing regular discussions, showing motivational videos, and assisting students with college admissions and financial aid in cities like Pune and Mumbai. Recently, Spark has initiated a learning space for children from the Vadar community, emphasizing education and environmental awareness to foster holistic growth and equal opportunities.

Web: https://www.linkedin.com/company/spark-social-education-and-welfare-society/posts/?feedView=all

Web Status : Functional but not consistent in efforts

2024-25.name: Mirtunjay Bharadwaj
2024-25.focus: Connecting children from slums and marginalized communities to mainstream society
2024-25.about:
Ramaamani Bhagvatsing Foundation, based in Begusarai, Bihar, works with community participation across sectors such as education, environment, road safety, sports, and healthcare. Its primary focus is on connecting children from slums and marginalized communities to mainstream society through a community-supported school, ensuring access to education and equal opportunities for growth.

Web: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mirtunjay-02725a2b8/

Web Status : Functional but not consistent in efforts

2024-25.name: Sanchit
2024-25.focus: Building bridges between Urdu and Marathi
2024-25.about:
The Shiksha Initiative focuses on enhancing early literacy by building the capacity of Anganwadi workers in Chhattisgarh to adopt an emergent literacy approach for teaching scripts to young children. The initiative develops contextual teaching and learning materials—including shared reading resources and read-aloud books—to make learning engaging and effective. By strengthening foundational literacy at the grassroots, the initiative aims to create a replicable model adaptable to Anganwadis across diverse regions of India.

Web: https://www.linkedin.com/in/sanchit-patne-3502ba185/?originalSubdomain=in

Web Status : NOT FUNCTIONAL - Working with different orgs

2024-25.name: Sarswati & Deepak
2024-25.about:
The STEP Foundation works for social change across urban, rural, and tribal areas of Nashik district, addressing issues related to education, health, environment, women's empowerment, youth, and farmers. The organization has conducted health awareness programs through street plays in over 70 government ashram schools, organized competitions to promote social values and artistic expression, and held workshops on waste management and constitutional awareness. STEP also runs 'Nai Talim' sessions to foster constitutional and human values among children and operates a vocational training center for women and a community library for youth, women, and children.

Web: https://www.linkedin.com/in/step-foundation-nashik-a87175320/

Web Status : Functional and consistent

2024-25.name: Vijay Kumar Paswan
2024-25.focus: Lighting up learning in Musahar communities
2024-25.about:
Aatmvishwas Foundation has been working in the areas of education and youth development for the past two years. The organization runs a Community Learning Center for children from the Musahar community, providing both basic and social education. Alongside this, Aatmvishwas works with youth to build self-confidence, create awareness of new opportunities, and nurture social leadership qualities.

Web: https://aatmvishwasfoundation.in/

Web Status : Functional and consistent

2024-25.name: Smita & Khushashri
2024-25.focus: From selling on Goa's beaches to dreaming beyond the waves
2024-25.about:
The organization works with children of laborers and migrant workers in Mapusa, Goa, who often drop out of schools in their home states due to frequent migration. Once out of school, returning becomes difficult because of financial constraints, language barriers, and social discrimination.
To address this, the organization creates child-friendly and inclusive learning spaces that keep children engaged through joyful, activity-based learning. These spaces help children rebuild confidence, nurture curiosity, and gradually reintegrate into the formal education system — giving them a fair chance at a brighter future.

Web Status : Not functional currently as works with migrant community

2024-25.name: Shubhangi & Vikas
2024-25.org: Mahamanav Baba Amte Bahuuddeshiya Samajik Vikas Seva Sanstha
2024-25.focus: From struggle to self-reliance, with compassion
2024-25.about:
Mahamanav Baba Amte Samajik Vikas Seva Sanstha, established in 2008 in Shrigonda, Ahmednagar, is a non-profit organization committed to community upliftment and social progress. Inspired by the values of transformative social leaders, the organization works to break the cycle of poverty by providing holistic support and quality education to marginalized children, especially from nomadic tribes.
By promoting inclusivity, skill development, and emotional well-being, the organization nurtures environments where every child can learn and grow. It also serves as a catalyst for community engagement—fostering empathy, awareness, and collective responsibility toward building a more compassionate society.

Web : https://aatmvishwasfoundation.in/

Web Status : Not functional currently as works with migrant community

2023-24.name: Amrut Kulkarni
2023-24.focus: Nurturing Hope and Learning in Potraj community
2023-24.about:
Swa Surajya Foundation works with children from the Potraj nomadic community through its Community Learning Centers (CLCs). These children often face barriers of migration, poverty, and exclusion from mainstream education.
At the CLCs, children engage in joyful daily learning, focusing on foundational literacy, creativity, and life skills that build confidence and expand their sense of possibility.
The initiative aims to break the cycle of generational poverty by providing Potraj children with consistent education, opportunities for self-expression, and a strong sense of community support. Alongside academics, the centers promote art, play, and skill development to nurture holistic growth and prepare children for a brighter, self-reliant future.

Web : https://www.linkedin.com/posts/panchkroshi-vidyalay_nomadic-educationforall-communitylearning-activity-7348761523349045249-gX_U?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_android&rcm=ACoAAASZwYEBo2NzyjajOHWx2P7XqyvvtbfHDjw

Web Status : Functional & Scaling

2023-24.name: Tejaswini Nageshwar
2023-24.focus: Creating Joyful Learning Spaces in Urban Slums
2023-24.about:
Garaj Foundation works in the Lakadganj and Dipty slums of Nagpur through Community Learning Centers (CLCs) focused on improving children's education and overall well-being.
The organization also works with nomadic communities living in urban slums, engaging pre-primary and primary-level children in experiential learning that promotes cognitive and academic development in a joyful, nurturing environment.
Beyond education, Garaj Foundation provides skill development, health services, and basic nutrition support for children of single parents, while also helping families access educational documents and essential resources. Active parental involvement is a key part of their approach, ensuring sustainable and holistic community development.

Web :https://garajfoundation.org/

Web Status : Functional

2023-24.name: Premdaya Kasadekar
2023-24.focus: Strengthening Learning in Korku Tribe living in Tiger Buffer Zones of Melghat
2023-24.about:
Sadaya Foundation works in the core regions of Melghat, Maharashtra, to strengthen primary education and foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) among children from the Korku tribal community. The organization also engages with parents to build awareness about the importance of education and their role in shaping their children's future.
Sadaya supports children before and after school, focusing on both academic and non-academic growth to ensure that every child attains foundational learning at the right age and develops curiosity to pursue higher education.
Alongside this, Sadaya works to improve school infrastructure and address language barriers by developing a Korku-Marathi curriculum, using contextual books and libraries to make learning more accessible and engaging.

Web Status : Functional & Scaling

2023-24.name: Santosh Khandare
2023-24.about:
Prarambh is a youth-led initiative from Nagpur that aims to utilize existing human potential and community skills to address social challenges. The group believes that individuals living on the streets, often seen as inactive or excluded from society, hold immense potential to contribute meaningfully when given the right opportunities.
Prarambh works with street children and families in the Lakadganj community, focusing on education and empowerment to help them become active, informed, and responsible members of society. Through learning and engagement, the initiative strives to transform lives and make marginalized communities self-reliant and socially included.

Web Status : Non Functional

2023-24.name: Prabhavati Patil
2023-24.about:
Maay Mati Foundation was established on 19 April 2022 with the spirit of honoring both the mother who gives life and the soil that sustains it. Alongside its sustainability initiatives, the foundation runs two key programs for children from nearby villages in Jalgaon.
Chando Bachi Shala focuses on providing quality education to rural children, while the Library Program spreads the joy of reading and cultivates a love for books. Through these efforts, Maay Mati Foundation strives to nurture learning, curiosity, and connection with one's roots among children in rural communities.

Web : https://www.linkedin.com/in/maay-mati-foundation-062868282/

Web Status : Functional

2023-24.name: Somnath Raut
2023-24.focus: Empowering Tribal Communities Through Education and Health
2023-24.about:
Rural and tribal areas of Thane District face multiple challenges, including poverty, unemployment, addiction, gender discrimination, child marriage, and poor access to education and healthcare. Due to a lack of awareness about rights and entitlements, government welfare schemes often fail to reach those living in extreme poverty.
To address these issues, Yuva Sanskar Bahuddeshiya Sanstha was founded with a vision to improve education and primary healthcare for children in the tribal belt of Kalyan-Murbad, Thane. The organization believes that education is the most powerful pathway to holistic and sustainable development, enabling children and their communities to build a brighter, more equitable future.

Web : https://www.yuvasanskar.org

Web Status : Functional

2022-23.name: Deepali Patil
2022-23.focus: Enabling Learning for children of sugar-cane cutters
2022-23.about:
Jidnyasa is a non-profit organization dedicated to improving educational opportunities for underprivileged rural children and the marginalized children of migrant sugarcane cutter families. The organization is committed to ensuring access to quality education and creating supportive, inclusive learning environments for these vulnerable communities.
Through its two flagship programs — AAS and AARAMBH — Jidnyasa provides safe and nurturing spaces where children can express themselves freely, explore their curiosity, and strengthen their foundational learning.
By breaking social and economic barriers, Jidnyasa works to ensure that every child has the opportunity to learn, grow, and realize their full potential.

Web : https://www.linkedin.com/in/deepali-patil-founder-of-jidnyasa-desire-to-develop-9a965345/

Web Status : Functional & Scalling

2022-23.name: Satyam Srivastava
2022-23.focus: Reimagining Learning for a Joyful and Meaningful Childhood
2022-23.about:
After closing his business in 2016 and spending three years exploring, learning, and unlearning beyond the mainstream education system, Satyam founded Chaitanya Kul, a not-for-profit organization rooted in the principles of human development and holistic learning. His work focuses on ensuring a safe, joyful childhood and a bright future for every child.
Chaitanya Kul creates learning environments that nurture peacefulness—harmony with oneself and the world—and usefulness, helping children grow into employable, compassionate, and contributing individuals.
The organization is currently conducting research to develop innovative learning models and pedagogies for children under 15 years, designed for use in primary schools and community learning spaces. With a long-term vision of expansion, Chaitanya Kul aims to establish one new learning space every year in rural areas.

Web Status : Functional & Running

2022-23.name: Mahesh Shinde
2022-23.focus: Creating safe spaces for children from vulnerable backgrounds
2022-23.about:
Mahesh is the founder of the Initiative for Socio-Economic and Educational Development Foundation (I-SEED Foundation), a non-profit organization established in 2017. I-SEED works to strengthen existing educational systems by providing academic guidance, sports support, mentorship for higher education, and scholarships for students in need.
Driven by a vision to bring socio-economic change and educational development among children, I-SEED focuses on those who require immediate support to continue their learning journey. Through its holistic approach, the organization strives to ensure that every child has the opportunity to learn, grow, and thrive.

Web Status : Functional but he is working somewhere else

2022-23.name: Akash Burrewar
2022-23.focus: Creating safe spaces for children from vulnerable backgrounds
2022-23.about:
An exposure visit to the Kolam Poda tribal belt during college deeply influenced the vision and direction of the organization. The team was moved by the challenges faced by the community—particularly the issue of unmarried mothers (Kumari Mata) and the lack of awareness around education, health, and child safety.
Recognizing these needs, the organization began its work in the villages of Kolam Podas in Ghatanji block, focusing on raising awareness about child marriage, promoting health and hygiene education, and conducting sessions on “good touch and bad touch” for children.
To ensure continued learning, learning centers were established in four villages, providing children access to quality education and a safe, nurturing space. Through these efforts, the organization aims to bring about lasting change in the lives of children and the broader Kolam Poda community.

Web : https://www.linkedin.com/in/mauli-bahuuddeshiya-sanstha-ghatanji-ngo-335134252/recent-activity

Status : Functional but not consistent

2022-23.name: Amol Mane
2022-23.about:
Seva Sadan is a multi-purpose social organization based in Kolhapur, working to improve the social, educational, economic, and health conditions of rural and low-income communities.
The organization strives to bring marginalized groups into the mainstream of development by enhancing their quality of life through various community-based initiatives. Guided by a strong sense of social commitment, Seva Sadan works toward inclusive growth and sustainable progress for all sections of society

2021-22.name: Pranali & Dhammanand
2021-22.focus: Empowering Dhangar and other Tribal Nomadic Communities Through Education
2021-22.about:
OVEE works toward the educational and social empowerment of citizens from tribal and nomadic communities in rural Maharashtra. Through its interventions in child education, health, and rights, the organization strives to create lasting, community-led change at the grassroots level. OVEE envisions a society where every child, regardless of socio-economic background, has access to quality, inclusive, and equitable education that nurtures intellectual, emotional, social, and physical growth. Its programs focus on promoting inclusive education, ensuring that every child learns without discrimination. At the same time, OVEE works to build an ecosystem that supports the holistic development of children by fostering human values, encouraging critical thinking, and providing opportunities for creative expression.

Web :https://www.oveetrust.org/

Web Status : Functional & Scalling

2021-22.name: Sahebrao Rathod
2021-22.focus: Empowering Phase-Pardhi Community Through Tanda (Hamlet) School
2021-22.about:
Zep Samaj Vikas Sanstha works in the drought-prone areas of Akola district in the Vidarbha region of Maharashtra, focusing on the upliftment of tribal and nomadic communities, child rights, and education. The organization recognizes that adverse social and economic conditions among nomadic tribes often hinder children's educational progress. To address this, Zep introduced an innovative concept called Tanda Shala (Hamlet Schooling) — a localized education model designed to reach children in their own settlements. Through this initiative, Zep ensures access to education for children from nomadic and tribal backgrounds while also emphasizing the development of both academic and life skills, helping them grow with confidence and resilience.

Web : https://zepmaharashtra.com/

Status : Functional

2021-22.name: Pallavi & Aditi
2021-22.focus: Cultivating a Love for Reading, One Child at a Time
2021-22.about:
Founded in 2014 in Nagpur, Reading Keeda Library began with just 150 books and three members, driven by a simple belief — every child deserves access to books, regardless of age, caste, gender, or background. Today, it has grown into a vibrant community library with over 7,000 books in English, Hindi, and Marathi, reaching nearly 500 children. Through engaging programs like Gammat Gaani Gaau Yaa for toddlers, Pitara Kahaniyon Ka for young readers, and an online Teens' Book Club connecting participants across India, Reading Keeda nurtures a lifelong love for reading. To extend its reach, the organization set up a library in an urban slum in Gurugram, offering 50 children from migrant families a safe and joyful reading space. In 2024, Reading Keeda launched "Kitabon ki Duniya"—a capacity-building initiative supporting educators and organizations in setting up children's libraries across tribal, rural, and urban areas.

Web : https://readingkeeda.org

Status : Functional & Running

2021-22.name: Neha Unahale
2021-22.focus: Creating Joyful and Inclusive Learning Spaces for children of agricultural labourers
2021-22.about:
CFLS (Centre for Learning Spaces) was founded with a vision that every child should have access to the best experiences, resources, and opportunities to grow into a compassionate and responsible human being. Working in Harda, Madhya Pradesh, CFLS is dedicated to making learning spaces joyful, inclusive, and child-centred. The organization focuses on transforming existing educational spaces to make them more inclusive while also creating alternative, child-friendly learning environments based on children's needs and interests. Alongside academics, CFLS works to build awareness and understanding of local social, economic, environmental, and political issues, helping children connect learning with real-life contexts. Currently, CFLS is also developing a collaborative learning space with a Zilla Parishad School, strengthening partnerships between communities and formal education systems.

Status : Working with her partner Tolesh

2021-22.name: Vineet Patil
2021-22.focus: Empowering 'failed' students and youth through community learning spaces
2021-22.about:
An engineer by qualification, Vineet began his career in the corporate sector before finding his true calling in the development space. He founded 'Ninavi Mitra', an initiative that created learning spaces for so-called “failed” students, women, and youth affected by addiction—helping them rebuild confidence and purpose through community-driven engagement. Currently, Vineet runs a community learning space in Chopda Tehsil, Jalgaon District (Maharashtra), reaching children from vulnerable backgrounds, particularly those from families of agricultural labourers and beggars.

As Program Director of Community Collectives at Vardhishnu, Vineet leads program design, workshop facilitation, and coordination efforts. He also plays a pivotal role as a coach and mentor to cohort members, guiding grassroots leaders in building sustainable, community-led change.

2020-21.name: Ajay Battulwar
2020-21.focus: Empowering Rural Youth Through Education and Support
2020-21.about:
In many small villages, education remains a distant dream due to uneducated parents, financial hardships, and lack of awareness about its importance. Children and adolescents suffer the most, missing out on opportunities to learn and grow. At Rudra Welfare Foundation, is dedicated to transforming rural youth through education, skill development, and holistic support. Their mission is to nurture responsible, compassionate, and empowered young leaders who drive sustainable change in their communities. Through initiatives like the annual Srijan Camp for adolescents aged 13-18, they promote creativity, self-discovery, and open conversations on real-life challenges. So far, they have reached 500+ families, strengthening parent-child relationships, promoting healthier lifestyles, and improving communication and career awareness. Beyond camps, they continue to support promising youth—offering financial, emotional, and social assistance to help them overcome barriers and lead with confidence and purpose.

Web Status : Only Running Srijan Camps at this moment
